paper_title: Many flows in the group connectivity setting
summary: Conjecture 1.10 from arXiv:2005.09767 asserts an exponential lower bound $c^n$ on the number of $\Gamma$-connected flows for every abelian group $\Gamma$ with $|\Gamma| \geq 6$. The source paper itself proves this for all groups of size $\geq 8$ (Theorem 1.9) and establishes only a superpolynomial (but sub-exponential) bound for $\mathbb{Z}_6$ (Theorem 1.11), leaving $\mathbb{Z}_6$ and $\mathbb{Z}_7$ open. No subsequent paper resolving the conjecture for these two groups was found in five targeted web searches across the literature through May 2026; the paper remains at a single arXiv version with no revision signalling a resolution.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch confirms this paper is about the square energy of graphs (spectral graph theory) and does not cite arXiv:2005.09767 or address group connectivity flows. The fuzz-match was a false positive; this reference is not relevant to the conjecture.

search_queries: arXiv 2005.09767 group connectivity flows exponential bound cite
3-edge-connected oriented graph abelian group flows exponential lower bound DeVos Mohar
notes: No follow-up paper resolving the conjecture for |Γ| = 6 or 7 was found. The conjecture is roughly 6 years old as of the review date, which is long enough that absence of evidence is somewhat suspicious but the two open cases (Z_6 and Z_7) are notoriously hard; confidence is set to medium rather than high. The sole internal reference candidate (2409.18220) was a false positive on the fuzz match.
